spec_text: All flanges shall be ASME B16.5 Class 300 rating minimum.
devicra_text: [DEVIATION] Customer requires ASME B16.5 Class 300 flanges. Manufacturer proposes JIS B2220 20K flanges as equivalent alternative based on equivalent pressure-temperature rating. Technical comparison table submitted separately. Request customer written approval.

spec_text: Drawing approval cycle: Vendor shall resubmit within 10 working days of receiving Purchaser's comments.
devicra_text: [DEVIATION] Standard drawing approval cycle of 10 working days is insufficient for complex multi-sheet assembly drawings (e.g., P&IDs, GA drawings). Manufacturer proposes 15 working days for documents with more than 5 sheets. Shorter cycle increases risk of incomplete review and re-submission loop.

spec_text: EN 10204 Type 3.2 for all pressure-containing parts and rotating components. Type 3.1 is not acceptable for rotor components.
devicra_text: [CLARIFICATION] Specification requires EN 10204 Type 3.2 for rotor components. Type 3.2 requires third-party validation of the material test report. Please confirm: (a) which third-party body is acceptable, and (b) whether the same TPI agency used for shop inspection can perform this validation.

spec_text: Rotor assembly shall be balanced to ISO 1940/1 Grade G1.0 (not G2.5).
devicra_text: [DEVIATION] Customer requires ISO G1.0 balance grade. Manufacturer's standard for this rotor class is G2.5 per API 617 requirement. Achieving G1.0 requires additional balancing time (+2 weeks) and specialized facility. Manufacturer proposes G2.5 as per API 617 minimum, or G1.0 at additional cost and schedule impact. Please advise.

spec_text: Purchaser's inspector shall have unrestricted access to the manufacturing facility at all times during the contract period.
devicra_text: [CLARIFICATION] 'Unrestricted access at all times' conflicts with Manufacturer's site security policy and controlled access to classified defense-related areas within the same facility. Please confirm acceptable access protocol: Manufacturer proposes formal entry request 24 hours in advance with escort for all visits.

spec_text: All test results shall be within the tolerances of API 610 Table 2. Any deviation shall require re-test after Purchaser approval.
devicra_text: [DEVIATION] API 610 Table 2 performance tolerances are accepted. However, mandatory re-test after any tolerance exceedance is impractical and causes significant schedule risk. Manufacturer proposes: minor deviations (within 5% of tolerance band) to be resolved by analysis and Purchaser agreement without physical re-test.

spec_text: Impellers shall be three-dimensional (3D) milled from a single billet of AISI 17-4PH (H1025 condition) stainless steel. No welded impellers.
devicra_text: [CLARIFICATION] Specification prohibits welded impellers. Please confirm: are electron-beam welded impellers (EBW, used for very narrow passage stages) also excluded? Manufacturer uses EBW for specific aerodynamic stages and this is proven technology. Clarification needed before finalizing impeller design.

spec_text: Noise level shall not exceed 85 dB(A) at 1 m from the package boundary under rated operating conditions. If this limit cannot be met, Vendor shall propose an acoustic enclosure.
devicra_text: [DEVIATION] Acoustic guarantee of 85 dB(A) at 1 m from package boundary under full-load conditions cannot be guaranteed for this compressor size without acoustic enclosure. Estimated actual level: 88–91 dB(A). Manufacturer proposes supply with acoustic enclosure at additional cost. Alternatively, customer may accept 90 dB(A) limit per ISO 1680 and rely on site hearing protection program.

spec_text: All external bolting shall be 316 stainless steel, minimum Grade B8.
devicra_text: [DEVIATION] 316SS B8 bolting for high-pressure flanged connections above 150°C may exhibit galling (seizure) during assembly and disassembly. Manufacturer proposes A193 B7 (alloy steel) with Teflon anti-seize coating for connections above 150°C, and B8M (316SS) for ambient temperature connections. Request written approval.

spec_text: All gas-side O-rings shall be of Viton (FKM) material. PTFE-encapsulated O-rings are not acceptable.
devicra_text: [CLARIFICATION] Specification requires FKM O-rings. For ethylene-rich gas service, certain FKM compounds are susceptible to explosive decompression (ED) failure. Manufacturer proposes HNBR (hydrogenated nitrile) as an alternative with proven ED resistance in ethylene service. Please confirm if HNBR is acceptable or provide FKM compound specification.

spec_text: Seal gas supply shall be at a pressure of at least 0.5 bar above the process gas pressure at the seal.
devicra_text: [CLARIFICATION] Specification defines 0.5 bar differential for seal gas supply. Please confirm: (a) this 0.5 bar delta-P is at the seal itself, not at the supply header, and (b) Purchaser confirms utility supply pressure stability to maintain this minimum delta-P during all operating transients including startup and shutdown.

spec_text: Lube oil reservoir shall be internally coated with two-component epoxy coating, minimum 200 microns DFT.
devicra_text: [DEVIATION] Internal epoxy coating of lube oil reservoir is contrary to Manufacturer's standard (uncoated, pickled stainless steel insert). Coating introduces risk of delamination particles contaminating lube oil system. Manufacturer proposes uncoated 316SS internal lining welded to carbon steel reservoir shell as alternative. Request approval.

spec_text: All instrument connection flanges shall be ASME B16.5 Class 300 RF. Thread connections are not acceptable for process measurements.
devicra_text: [DEVIATION] ASME Class 300 flanged connections for all process instruments significantly increase cost and weight. Manufacturer's standard practice: Class 300 flanges for process connections ≥ 2\ and in H2S service; NPT threaded for ≤ 1\" utility service (e.g.
category: vent). Request approval of this hybrid approach."

spec_text: String test (compressor + gearbox + driver assembled) shall be conducted.
devicra_text: [CLARIFICATION] Specification requires string test. Please confirm: (a) steam supply conditions available at Manufacturer's test facility (42 barg / 400°C required; available: 35 barg / 360°C maximum), (b) if steam conditions are lower than design, is performance correction by calculation acceptable, (c) Purchaser's required attendance duration for string test.

spec_text: Lube oil cleanliness shall meet ISO 4406 cleanliness level 16/14/11 minimum.
devicra_text: [DEVIATION] ISO 4406 level 16/14/11 is extremely clean and requires extended flushing time (estimated 3–5 days per train) with multiple oil changes. This adds approximately 2 weeks to the test schedule. Manufacturer proposes 17/15/12 per API 614 minimum, which is proven adequate for journal bearings of this type. If 16/14/11 is required, schedule impact of +14 days must be approved.

spec_text: No asbestos-containing materials shall be used in any part of the package.
devicra_text: [CLARIFICATION] Manufacturer confirms no asbestos is used in any currently procured materials. However, some sub-vendor supplied components (e.g., legacy seal gaskets from long-standing sub-vendors in Japan) may historically have used asbestos-reinforced compounds. Please confirm if a general declaration of conformance from Manufacturer is sufficient, or if individual component-level certificates are required.

spec_text: Vendor shall submit a Level 3 manufacturing schedule in Primavera P6 or Microsoft Project format within 21 days of order.
devicra_text: [DEVIATION] Manufacturer uses an in-house production scheduling system (ERP-based Gantt chart, Excel export only). Full Primavera P6 integration is not available. Manufacturer proposes submission of Level 3 schedule as Excel Gantt chart with all critical path milestones clearly identified. Monthly update reports will be in the same format.

spec_text: Turning gear shall be supplied for cool-down rotation after shutdown.
devicra_text: [CLARIFICATION] Turning gear is listed as required but is not included in the equipment scope list (Section 1.1). Please confirm: (a) is turning gear in Vendor scope, (b) required rotation speed (typically 1–5 RPM), (c) is air motor or electric motor drive preferred. Manufacturer will include if confirmed in scope.

spec_text: Maximum individual lift weight: 30 metric tons. If the package exceeds this limit, Vendor shall propose a lifting arrangement.
devicra_text: [DEVIATION] Estimated assembled package weight is 38 metric tons, exceeding the 30-ton limit. Manufacturer proposes to ship the compressor body and base frame separately (max 22 tons each) with bolted connections assembled on site. Vendor will supply detailed assembly procedure and site alignment procedure. Request approval of this shipping split.

spec_text: Primary seal gas flow: maximum 50 Nm³/h per seal at rated conditions.
devicra_text: [DEVIATION] Based on thermodynamic calculation at rated conditions, estimated primary seal gas consumption is 55–60 Nm³/h per seal for this casing size. Manufacturer proposes 65 Nm³/h as contract guarantee value. Exceeding 50 Nm³/h limit means Purchaser must confirm utility supply capacity is adequate. Request discussion and revised limit.

spec_text: Governor shall be an electronic speed governor (ESG) with remote speed setpoint capability via 4-20 mA signal from DCS.
devicra_text: [CLARIFICATION] DCS interface for remote speed setpoint requires confirmation of: (a) signal range: 4 mA = minimum governor speed, 20 mA = maximum speed (or reverse), (b) fail-safe action on signal loss: speed hold, run-back to minimum, or trip. Manufacturer to wire as confirmed. Please advise before control system detailed design.

spec_text: Required delivery date: within 28 weeks from the date of Purchase Order.
devicra_text: [DEVIATION] 28-week delivery for a complete API 610 pump package including performance test with Purchaser witness is not achievable from order placement. Minimum realistic schedule is 34 weeks including all engineering approvals, long-lead material procurement (motor: 14 weeks, shaft: 10 weeks, mechanical seal: 12 weeks), and testing. Manufacturer requests schedule relaxation or early order for long-lead items.

spec_text: Cooling water inlet: 32°C / outlet: maximum 45°C.
devicra_text: [CLARIFICATION] Please confirm cooling water design fouling factor used for this site. Manufacturer will use TEMA standard fouling factor unless site-specific value is specified. Also confirm cooling water chemistry (TDS, pH, biological treatment) to allow proper tube and tubesheet material selection.

spec_text: All welding procedures and welder qualifications shall be submitted for Purchaser's review before commencement of welding.
devicra_text: [CLARIFICATION] Manufacturer has over 300 active WPS/WPQRs on file. Submitting all WPS documents before welding commences would require significant engineering time and create bottlenecks. Manufacturer proposes: submit a Welding Procedure Register (list of all applicable WPS with reference numbers) first, then submit individual WPS on demand for specific weld joints of concern. Request approval of this phased approach.

spec_text: Vendor shall confirm motor shaft dimensions within 14 days of order to allow motor procurement in parallel.
devicra_text: [DEVIATION] Motor shaft dimensions are defined by the motor manufacturer and are confirmed at motor design freeze, typically 8–10 weeks after order placement — not within 14 days. Manufacturer can provide estimated shaft dimensions within 14 days based on typical motors for this power rating, but final confirmation is only possible at 10 weeks. Customer should note this in motor procurement schedule.

spec_text: All lifting attachments (lugs, trunnions) shall be proof-tested to 150% of maximum lift weight and certified by an independent inspection body.
devicra_text: [DEVIATION] Proof testing lifting lugs on assembled equipment at 150% of load creates significant risk of structural damage to the package itself. Manufacturer's standard practice: lugs proof-tested to 125% per JIS B8817, with FEA calculation report submitted for certification. Request acceptance of FEA-based certification as equivalent to physical proof test at 150%.

spec_text: Third-party inspection by Purchaser's nominated TPI agency shall be accommodated at all stages of manufacture at no additional cost.
devicra_text: [CLARIFICATION] 'No additional cost' is understood to mean no extra charge for reasonable access and cooperation with the TPI agency. However, if TPI requests destructive testing (cross-sectioning, destructive metallurgical checks) beyond the ITP scope, Manufacturer will submit a cost variation. Please confirm understanding is aligned.

spec_text: Fouling factors: TEMA Tables 5.1 and 5.2, plus an additional 25% margin on the calculated dirty U-value.
devicra_text: [DEVIATION] Additional 25% margin on dirty U-value results in over-sized coolers, higher pressure drop, and increased package footprint. Cooler outlet temperature may fall below process dew point, causing condensation in the gas path. Manufacturer proposes standard TEMA fouling factors without additional margin and requests process engineer review of minimum gas temperature requirements.

spec_text: Commissioning spares for 2 years of continuous operation shall be included in the scope.
devicra_text: [CLARIFICATION] Specification requires 2-year commissioning spares as part of base scope. Please confirm: (a) is this in addition to the 24-month operational spare parts list submitted at bid stage, (b) what is the definition of 'commissioning spares' — does it include consumables replaced during first startup (gaskets, lubricants, filter elements) or only spares for failures?

spec_text: ISO 45001 (or OHSAS 18001) and ISO 14001 certifications shall be submitted at bid.
devicra_text: [DEVIATION] Manufacturer holds ISO 14001 and ISO 9001 certifications. ISO 45001 certification is currently in progress (expected certification date: Q3 2026). Manufacturer will submit ISO 45001 gap analysis report and implementation schedule at bid stage as evidence of commitment. Request acceptance of this interim arrangement pending full certification.
